title: Luke and Leia
content:
{{Top|music}} {{Youmay|the musical theme|[[Luke & Leia|the magazine article]]}} {{Update|{{YouTube|text=Luke & Leia (From "Star Wars: Return of the Jedi")|channel=channel/UCpNCpiKSyPaItvtb18V_vxQ|name=The Vienna Philharmonic Orchestra - Topic|archivedate=20210711212222|url=p8UoScKNC6Q}}}} {{Music |image=[[File:Luke-and-Leia.png]] |title="Luke and Leia" |composer=[[John Williams]] |written=[[1983]] |recorded= |release date= |represents=*Reunion **The [[Parent|siblings]] [[Luke Skywalker|Luke]] and [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Skywalker]] |performedby=[[London Symphony Orchestra]] |genre=Film score |length= |key=D-flat major |tempo= 𝅘𝅥 = 78 |texture= |timesig=4/4 |meter= |mode= |lyrics= |soundtracks= |movies=[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|VI]], [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|III]], [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|VIII]], [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|IX]] |EU=*''LEGO Star Wars'' {{C|[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game|I]], [[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy|II]]}} *[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast|''Jedi Outcast'']] *[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance|''X-Wing Alliance'']] |canon= |media={{Audio|Luke and Leia.flac|"Luke and Leia"}} }} '''"Luke and Leia"''' is a musical theme composed by [[John Williams]] for the [[Parent|siblings]] [[Luke Skywalker|Luke]] and [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Skywalker]]. The piece was written for the film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], and is given a full statement during the Skywalker siblings' conversation in [[Bright Tree Village]] about their [[Skywalker family|familial bonds]]. ==Conception and development== {{Quote|George Lucas—certainly a genius. George, you've given me the greatest opportunity—the broadest canvas—to write themes for characters. Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader, Anakin, Luke and Leia, the Force, and so on. For the first film, George, I even wrote—you'll remember—a quite heated love theme with a melody and a development section, and a torrid climax—thinking that Luke and Leia were lovers. And I found out two years later—that they were brother and sister.|John Williams|}} [[John Williams]] initially composed a "quite heated" love theme for [[Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Princess Leia]] for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] to the extent of writing a development section that led up to a "torrid" climax. However, the theme never materialized for the [[1977]] film, and Williams later discovered that Luke and Leia were [[Parent|siblings]] only years later.{{YouTube|video=JTmcmxl2OpQ|text=John Williams accepts the 44th AFI Life Achievement Award|channel=@afi|name=American Film Institute|archiveurl=https://archive.is/U5fac}} Nevertheless, for the third installment of [[George Lucas]]' [[Original trilogy|original ''Star Wars'' trilogy]], [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], John Williams wrote "Luke and Leia" for the [[Skywalker family|family]] relationship between the two characters. A full statement of the theme was included in the film, which was released in [[1983]]. For the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] release of ''Return of the Jedi'', Williams composed the concert piece "[[Victory Celebration]]," which was a rendition of "Luke and Leia." ==Usage== {{Quote|Yes. It's you, Leia.''"
"''I know. Somehow… I've always known.''"
"''Then you know why I have to face him.''"
"''No! Luke, run away. Far away. If he can feel your presence then leave this place. I wish I could go with you.''"
"''No you don't. You've always been strong.|"Luke and Leia" plays as Luke and Leia converse|{{Film|VI}}}} [[File:Luke_Leia_BTV_RotJ.png|thumb|left|270px|Luke and Leia]] "Luke and Leia" first appeared in the film ''Return of the Jedi'', where a full statement of the piece is played when Luke and Leia converse at [[Bright Tree Village]] about their shared family relationship and once more directly after the second Death Star is destroyed when Leia tells Han she is Luke's sister. A small part of the theme is used in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] when Anakin talks with Padmé about his vision. The theme reappears in [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VIII ''The Last Jedi'']], when Luke discusses how [[Han Solo]] and Leia left their [[Parent|son]], [[Ben Solo]], under his tutelage. The motif then transitions to "[[Han Solo and the Princess]]." A full statement of the Skywalker siblings' leitmotif returns when Luke and Leia reunite at the [[Crait outpost]]. "Luke and Leia" is also played in [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']], in the track "[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (soundtrack)|Reunion]]." The beginning of the track is a faster rendition of a [[Wikipedia:Phrase (music)|phrase]] from "Luke and Leia," which transitions into the "[[Star Wars Main Title]]" and later "[[The Force Theme]]" as [[starship]]s of the [[Citizens' Fleet]] gather on [[Ajan Kloss]]. After playing several other themes, the melody of "Luke and Leia" is played, at which point the screenplay depicts [[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian]] and [[Jannah]] conversing. The theme is also played in the ''Bond Unbroken'' music video on the ''[[Star Wars: A Musical Journey]]'' DVD. The piece is also played in ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]]'', ''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''. Additionally, an extract of the theme features prominently in the opening credits of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''. In the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)]] it is often used as a leitmotif for [[Nomi Sunrider]]. ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{YouTube|text=John Williams Plays "Luke & Leia" Theme on Piano|channel=channel/UCLwccpA85GCgdjAQB53OQXQ|name=Frank Lehman|quote=Audio excerpt from 1983 Gene Shalit interview with John Williams, with composer previewing new music from Return of the Jedi on piano.|archivedate=20211009140957|url=gvzGPVpP95k}} *{{YouTube|text=Luke & Leia (From "Star Wars: Return of the Jedi")|channel=channel/UCpNCpiKSyPaItvtb18V_vxQ|name=The Vienna Philharmonic Orchestra - Topic|archivedate=20210711212222|url=p8UoScKNC6Q}} {{Themes|saga=1}} {{RWM}} [[Category:Themes and leitmotifs]]
